children.description: useRouteError returns the thrown value inside an ErrorBoundary, and isRouteErrorResponse distinguishes between thrown Response objects (expected errors like 404) and unexpected JavaScript errors. Treating all errors identically shows generic messages for expected 404s and leaks stack traces for unexpected ones.

children.title: throw new Response() for Expected Errors
children.description: Throwing a Response with a 4xx status from a loader communicates expected error conditions (not found, forbidden) to the ErrorBoundary without triggering unexpected error paths. Returning null for missing resources instead causes components to render with undefined data, producing cryptic type errors rather than clean error UIs.

children.title: 404 Handling with Splat Routes
children.description: A splat route file ($.tsx) catches all unmatched URLs and renders a custom 404 page. Without it, Remix falls through to the root ErrorBoundary for not-found URLs, showing a generic error instead of a helpful 'page not found' experience that preserves site navigation.

children.title: Retry Logic and Exponential Backoff
children.description: External API calls from Remix loaders should implement retry with exponential backoff for transient failures. Immediate re-throwing of network errors without retry causes unnecessary user-facing failures for temporary outages, while retrying without backoff floods failing services and worsens their recovery time.

children.title: Timeout Patterns with AbortController
children.description: Loader fetch calls should use AbortController with setTimeout to enforce maximum response times. Without timeouts, a slow external service holds the Remix server request open indefinitely, consuming connection pool slots and eventually causing cascading failures across all concurrent requests.

children.title: Structured Error Logging on the Server
children.description: Errors caught in Remix loaders and actions should be logged with structured context (user ID, route, request ID) before being rethrown or converted to user-facing responses. Logging only error messages without context makes production debugging near-impossible when errors are intermittent.

children.title: entry.server.tsx and handleError
children.description: Remix's entry.server.tsx handleError export is the global catch point for unhandled server errors. Connecting it to an error monitoring service (like Sentry) here ensures all unexpected crashes are captured before they are swallowed by the error boundary rendering path.

remix-dev.title: Advanced Data Patterns
remix-dev.description: This layer covers advanced fetching, caching, and optimistic patterns. These techniques make Remix apps feel fast and stay in sync.
remix-dev.challenge: Add inline editing with useFetcher and optimistic updates to the contacts list.

children.title: Revalidation and shouldRevalidate
children.description: Remix revalidates all active loaders after any action by default. Exporting shouldRevalidate from a route module allows skipping unnecessary refetches for routes whose data cannot have changed. Disabling revalidation incorrectly causes UI to show stale data after mutations, creating subtle data consistency bugs.

children.title: useLoaderData vs useRouteLoaderData
children.description: useRouteLoaderData accesses data from any ancestor route's loader by route ID, enabling deeply nested components to consume parent loader data without prop drilling. Using useLoaderData in a component that is not a direct route module throws a runtime error because there is no associated loader in that context.

children.title: Pagination with cursor-based vs offset
children.description: Cursor-based pagination (using a unique field as a cursor) scales to millions of rows without degrading query performance. Offset-based pagination using skip/take causes full table scans that grow linearly with page depth, making page 500 take orders of magnitude longer than page 1.

children.title: N+1 Queries and include vs select
children.description: Prisma's include fetches relations in separate queries joined in application code, which can become an N+1 problem in loops. Using select with nested relation fields or Prisma's fluent API consolidates queries, preventing performance cliffs where listing 100 items triggers 101 database round-trips.

children.title: Zod for Schema Validation
children.description: Zod validates and parses FormData or JSON from actions and loaders at runtime, ensuring server data matches TypeScript types. Trusting TypeScript types alone without runtime validation means malformed or malicious inputs reach database queries unchecked, enabling injection attacks or corrupted records.

children.title: conform for Type-Safe Forms
children.description: The conform library bridges Zod schemas and HTML forms with accessible error binding and progressive enhancement support. Building custom form validation without it leads to inconsistent error message patterns, missing ARIA attributes for screen readers, and validation that only works with JavaScript enabled.

children.title: Row-Level Security via Loader Guards
children.description: Every loader that fetches user-specific data must scope Prisma queries to the authenticated user's ID. Fetching by resource ID alone without verifying ownership allows horizontal privilege escalation, where any logged-in user can read or modify any other user's data by guessing IDs.

children.title: Optimistic Concurrency with version Fields
children.description: Adding a version integer to Prisma models enables optimistic locking: update queries include WHERE version = $current, and zero affected rows signals a conflict. Without concurrency control, concurrent edits silently overwrite each other, producing last-write-wins data loss in collaborative features.

remix-dev.title: Styling and Performance
remix-dev.description: This layer covers building a responsive UI and optimizing performance. A clean, fast interface makes the application feel professional.
remix-dev.challenge: Apply a responsive Tailwind design and tune the app for strong Web Vitals.

children.title: Tailwind Setup and PostCSS Integration
children.description: Remix integrates Tailwind through PostCSS, which processes utility classes during the Vite build. Importing the Tailwind CSS file in root.tsx's links export rather than as a direct import ensures proper server-side rendering of styles without FOUC on initial page load.

children.title: CSS Custom Properties and Theming
children.description: Tailwind v4 uses CSS custom properties for the design token system, enabling runtime theming without JavaScript. Hardcoding colors as arbitrary Tailwind values instead of referencing theme tokens makes consistent dark mode and brand theming across a large codebase extremely difficult to maintain.

children.title: links() Export and Stylesheet Preloading
children.description: Route modules export a links() function that injects link tags including stylesheet preload hints for critical CSS. Styles added only via JavaScript are render-blocking and increase cumulative layout shift scores; using the links export ensures CSS is delivered in the initial HTML response.

children.title: Code Splitting and Route-Based Chunks
children.description: Remix automatically code-splits at route boundaries, so each route's JavaScript is only loaded when needed. Importing large libraries at the module level in a parent route pushes those bytes to all child routes, increasing initial bundle size and Time to Interactive for pages that don't use those libraries.

children.title: Largest Contentful Paint and SSR
children.description: Remix's server-side rendering delivers a complete HTML document on the first request, dramatically improving LCP compared to client-rendered SPAs. Re-introducing useEffect-driven data fetching for the primary page content defeats this advantage and regresses to SPA waterfall performance characteristics.

children.title: ScrollRestoration and Navigation UX
children.description: Remix's ScrollRestoration component preserves scroll position across client-side navigations and restores it correctly on back/forward navigation. Without it, users navigating back from a detail page always return to the top of a long list, creating disorienting UX that violates browser navigation expectations.

children.title: Static Assets in the public Directory
children.description: Files in Remix's public directory are served with long-lived cache headers and fingerprinted URLs by Vite. Referencing assets with hardcoded paths outside public bypasses fingerprinting, causing browsers to serve stale cached versions after a deployment that updates those assets.

children.title: Responsive Images with srcset
children.description: Serving appropriately sized images using srcset and sizes attributes reduces bandwidth on mobile devices and improves LCP scores. Serving a single large desktop image to all screen sizes is one of the most impactful performance mistakes, as images often account for 60-80% of total page weight.

remix-dev.title: Testing and Deployment
remix-dev.description: This layer covers testing the app and deploying it to production. Tests and an adapter-based deploy make the project a real, reliable product.
remix-dev.challenge: Add tests and deploy the Remix contacts app with a hosted database.

children.title: Testing Loaders and Actions in Isolation
children.description: Remix loaders and actions are plain async functions that accept Request objects, making them directly testable with Vitest without a running server. Skipping unit tests for loaders and testing only through the browser means subtle data transformation bugs and auth guard failures go undetected until end-to-end tests run, significantly slowing the feedback loop.

children.title: Mocking Prisma with Vitest
children.description: Using vitest.mock() with a Prisma mock library (like prisma-mock or jest-mock-extended) allows testing loader/action logic against controlled database responses. Tests that hit a real database are slow, order-dependent, and fail non-deterministically in CI environments that spin up fresh containers without seeded data.

children.title: React Testing Library with Remix Wrappers
children.description: Components that use Remix hooks (useLoaderData, useNavigate) must be wrapped in a MemoryRouter with route context when tested in isolation. Rendering them without context causes hooks to throw invariant errors, blocking the entire test suite rather than just the failing component.

children.title: Accessibility Testing with axe-core
children.description: Integrating jest-axe or vitest-axe into component tests catches WCAG violations automatically on every commit. Deferring accessibility checks to manual QA means violations accumulate until they represent a costly retrofit, and screen reader users encounter broken experiences in production.

children.title: Playwright Test Setup and Fixtures
children.description: Playwright fixtures enable reusable setup logic like authenticated user state, seeded database records, and consistent browser contexts. Writing auth logic inline in every test file creates massive duplication, and a single change to the login flow requires updating dozens of test files simultaneously.

children.title: Page Object Model Pattern
children.description: The Page Object Model encapsulates page-specific selectors and interactions into reusable classes, decoupling tests from HTML structure. Tests that hardcode CSS selectors or text strings throughout break whenever UI is redesigned, requiring rewrites of entire test suites rather than a single class update.

children.title: Remix Server Adapters
children.description: Remix uses adapter packages to target different deployment environments: @remix-run/express for Node.js servers, @remix-run/cloudflare for edge workers, and platform-specific adapters for Vercel and Fly.io. Using the wrong adapter causes the server entry point to fail silently or produce incorrect response handling.

children.title: Environment Variables and .env Files
children.description: Remix exposes only variables prefixed for public use to the browser; all others remain server-only. Accessing process.env in client-side code throws at runtime in production even if it works in development, and accidentally exposing secret keys in client bundles is a critical security vulnerability.
